title: Companies in the US Personal Data Supply Chain
subtitle: Who Monetizes, Sells, or Aggregates Consumer Data

schema_notes.tier: wholesale = B2B brokers operating in the background; retail = consumer-facing companies that also monetize data; both = operates in both tiers
schema_notes.category: Functional category within the data supply chain

categories.name: Credit Bureaus & Consumer Reporting Agencies (CRAs)
categories.definition: Credit bureaus are the original data brokers. They collect financial behavior data from lenders, then package and resell it to creditors, insurers, employers, landlords, and marketers. Governed by FCRA, but significant marketing-arm flows occur outside FCRA scope.

companies.name: Equifax
companies.description: One of the 'big three' CRAs; ~220M US consumer files; credit reports, scores, identity verification, fraud analytics; also operates The Work Number.
notable_actions: $575M FTC/CFPB settlement (2019) for 2017 breach affecting 147M people

companies.name: Experian
companies.description: Big three CRA; credit files, fraud prevention, identity verification; Experian Marketing Services sells profiles outside FCRA scope.

companies.name: TransUnion
companies.description: Big three CRA; credit reporting, fraud prevention; acquired Neustar in 2021 for $3.1B to expand into marketing and identity resolution.

companies.name: Innovis
companies.description: Fourth major CRA, less prominent than big three; used for credit decisions and fraud screening.

companies.name: LexisNexis Risk Solutions
companies.description: Operates C.L.U.E. (Comprehensive Loss Underwriting Exchange) for insurance claims history; identity verification, fraud, public records across insurance, financial services, and government; works with 98 of top 100 US personal lines carriers.

companies.name: Cotality (formerly CoreLogic)
companies.description: Property data, mortgage analytics, specialty insurance scores, and CLUE property claims; rebranded from CoreLogic in March 2025.
notable_actions: Rebranded from CoreLogic to Cotality in March 2025

companies.name: Verisk Analytics
companies.description: Parent of ISO, A-PLUS (Auto/Property claims exchange), AIR Worldwide (catastrophe modeling); major data analytics hub for insurance and financial services.

companies.name: MIB Group
companies.description: Founded 1902; coded medical and non-medical data sharing for life, health, disability underwriting; covers 99% of individual life policies.

companies.name: ChexSystems
companies.description: Consumer banking history CRA; banks use it to screen account applicants for overdrafts, fraud, forced closures.

companies.name: Equifax Workforce Solutions / The Work Number
companies.description: Centralized employment and income verification database; payroll records from 2M+ employers; covers 50%+ of US workers; used by lenders, landlords, government.

companies.name: NCTUE (National Consumer Telecom & Utilities Exchange)
companies.description: Specialty CRA tracking utility and telecom payment history for consumers with thin credit files.

companies.name: Early Warning Services
companies.description: Operates Zelle and the Early Warning bank fraud database; CRA for deposit account screening.

categories.name: People-Search / Identity Data Brokers
categories.definition: Brokers compiling publicly available info (voter rolls, court records, property, social media) combined with commercial data to build consumer identity profiles sold to anyone who pays. FTC's 2014 study found the nine largest had 3,000 data segments on nearly every US consumer.

companies.name: Acxiom / LiveRamp
companies.hq: Little Rock, AR / San Francisco, CA
companies.description: Acxiom maintains ~2.5B consumer profiles globally; LiveRamp operates identity resolution linking offline/online identities.

companies.name: Epsilon
companies.description: Consumer database of ~250M US profiles; powers direct mail, email, digital targeting for Fortune 500.

companies.name: Spokeo
companies.description: Consumer-facing people search aggregating public records, social profiles, contact information.

companies.name: Intelius
companies.description: People-search platform selling background checks, phone lookups, identity reports directly to consumers.

companies.name: BeenVerified
companies.description: Consumer-facing people-search and background check platform.

companies.name: Whitepages
companies.description: Longstanding directory and identity platform; operates Hiya (caller ID) and TenantCheck.

companies.name: PeopleFinders
companies.description: People-search site aggregating public records and contact data.

companies.name: Radaris
companies.description: People-search engine aggregating public and commercial data.

companies.name: MyLife
companies.description: Consumer-facing profile aggregation with 'reputation scores.'
notable_actions: FTC and state AG scrutiny for deceptive reputation score practices

companies.name: TruePeopleSearch
companies.description: Free people-search aggregating public records; monetized via advertising.

categories.name: Marketing / Audience Data Brokers & Data Enrichment
categories.definition: Brokers selling demographic, psychographic, purchase-intent, and behavioral consumer segments to advertisers, political campaigns, direct marketers. Often outside FCRA because data isn't used for credit or employment decisions.

companies.name: Neustar / TransUnion Marketing Solutions
companies.description: Identity resolution, audience targeting, marketing analytics; OneID and TrueAudience platforms.

companies.name: Merkle
companies.description: Data-driven performance marketing agency with proprietary consumer identity graph for CRM onboarding and digital targeting.

companies.name: Data Axle (formerly Infogroup)
companies.description: Business and consumer database marketing; B2B and B2C contact lists, data enrichment, digital audience segments.

companies.name: V12 Data
companies.description: Automotive and consumer data for targeted marketing; merged with Windfall Data in 2023.

companies.name: Datalogix
companies.description: Offline purchase data for digital targeting; part of Oracle Advertising which was largely wound down in 2023.
notable_actions: Oracle Advertising wound down in 2023

companies.name: Nielsen
companies.description: TV audience measurement, consumer panel (Nielsen Homescan), marketing analytics; sells audience segments for programmatic activation.

companies.name: Claritas
companies.description: PRIZM segmentation system (68 lifestyle and lifestage clusters); consumer demographic and behavioral segments.

companies.name: Experian Marketing Services
companies.description: Marketing arm of Experian; consumer data lists, email append, digital audience targeting outside FCRA scope.

categories.name: Ad-Tech & Programmatic Advertising
categories.definition: Ad-tech enables real-time automated buying/selling of digital ads. Built on cross-site/cross-app surveillance of browsing, purchase intent, and demographics for micro-targeting.

companies.name: Google / Alphabet
companies.description: Dominates search, display, video advertising; DV360 (DSP), Google Ad Manager (SSP); Chrome and Android as data infrastructure; ~80% of Alphabet revenue is ads.

companies.name: Meta Platforms
companies.description: Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp ads; cross-site tracking via Facebook Pixel and Conversions API; ~97% ads revenue.
notable_actions: Multiple GDPR fines in EU

companies.name: Amazon Advertising
companies.description: Leverages purchase/intent signals from 200M+ US Prime members; $56.2B ad revenue in 2024.

companies.name: The Trade Desk
companies.description: Leading independent DSP; operates UID2.0 open identity (built on emails) as cookie alternative.

companies.name: Criteo
companies.description: Commerce media platform specializing in retargeting and retail media.

companies.name: Magnite
companies.description: Largest independent SSP after Rubicon Project + Telaria merger; CTV and display exchange infrastructure.

companies.name: PubMatic
companies.description: Independent sell-side advertising platform managing publisher inventory across display, video, CTV.

companies.name: Index Exchange
companies.description: SSP providing programmatic marketplace infrastructure.

companies.name: LiveRamp
companies.description: Identity resolution infrastructure connecting first-party data across the ad-tech ecosystem via RampID.

companies.name: ID5
companies.description: Shared cookieless identity solution for publishers and ad-tech, replacing third-party cookies.

companies.name: Lotame
companies.description: Independent DMP offering audience data and Panorama ID identity solution.

categories.name: Location Data Brokers
categories.definition: Harvest precise GPS coordinates from mobile app SDKs (often via apps with unrelated location permissions). Package as audience segments, movement patterns, POI visits sold to advertisers, hedge funds, government. 2024 FTC enforcement wave heavily disrupted this sector.

companies.name: X-Mode Social / Outlogic
companies.description: Embedded SDKs in hundreds of apps to harvest location, including apps used by Muslim communities.
notable_actions: FTC ordered deletion of all historical location data and prohibited sale of sensitive location data (Jan 9, 2024)

companies.name: Gravy Analytics
companies.description: Tracked visits to health clinics, places of worship, political events.
notable_actions: FTC action Dec 3, 2024; required to destroy historical data

companies.name: Venntel
companies.description: Sold location data to US government agencies including DHS, CBP, IRS.
notable_actions: Covered by Dec 2024 FTC order against Gravy/Venntel

companies.name: Mobilewalla
companies.description: Collected 500M+ unique consumer IDs; harvested location data from RTB auctions without bidding.
notable_actions: FTC action Dec 3, 2024; first-ever allegation that RTB harvesting without bidding is an unfair practice

companies.name: SafeGraph
companies.description: POI visit data and foot traffic analytics for hedge funds, research, advertisers.
notable_actions: Temporarily suspended government data sales in 2021 under public pressure

companies.name: Veraset
companies.description: Precise location data and mobility analytics; spun out from SafeGraph.

companies.name: Foursquare
companies.description: Location intelligence platform combining POI, panel movement, attribution analytics.

companies.name: Cuebiq
companies.description: Location intelligence and foot traffic analytics for CPG, retail, OOH measurement.

companies.name: Near Intelligence
companies.description: Global location data platform covering 1.6B+ devices; financial difficulties through 2024.
notable_actions: Financial difficulties; restructuring through 2024

companies.name: Anomaly Six
companies.description: Defense contractor and commercial location data broker supplying US intelligence community.

companies.name: Babel Street
companies.description: Government-facing intelligence analytics; major DHS, FBI, DOD contractor.

companies.name: Kochava
companies.description: Mobile measurement and attribution platform; alleged sale of sensitive location data from hundreds of millions of devices.
notable_actions: FTC sued Aug 2022; amended complaint July 2024; case pending

companies.name: PlaceIQ
companies.description: Location analytics and foot traffic measurement; rebranded under Near.

companies.name: Tamoco
companies.description: Mobile location data platform for audience targeting and attribution.

companies.name: Complementics
companies.description: Location-based audience data using SDK and carrier partnerships.

categories.name: Health & Wellness Data
categories.definition: Health data brokers operate in a HIPAA gap. HIPAA covers only 'covered entities' and business associates. De-identified data, wellness apps, prescription discount programs, and DTC genetic testing operate largely outside HIPAA.

companies.name: IQVIA
companies.description: Largest health data company in the world; 90-93% of US retail pharmacy claims; real-world evidence to pharma, biotech, insurers.

companies.name: Komodo Health
companies.description: Maps 330M patient journeys across 65B clinical events for pharma market access and clinical operations.

companies.name: Datavant
companies.description: Health data logistics; connects 70,000+ hospitals/clinics and 70% of health system data via tokenization.

companies.name: HealthVerity
companies.description: Healthcare data marketplace with 75+ real-world data sources for pharma and life sciences buyers.

companies.name: Truveta
companies.description: De-identified EHR data pooled from member health systems sold to pharma and life sciences.

companies.name: Trilliant Health
companies.description: Healthcare market analytics and consumer health data for payers, providers, investors.

companies.name: 23andMe
companies.description: Consumer DTC genetic testing with 15M+ customers; ~80% consented to research use.
notable_actions: Filed bankruptcy March 2025; court-approved sale June 2025 to TTAM (nonprofit led by co-founder Anne Wojcicki) for $305M after public outcry over Regeneron's $256M bid

companies.name: Ancestry
companies.description: DNA and genealogy database with 22M+ test customers; monetizes data for research partnerships under consent.

companies.name: GoodRx
companies.description: Prescription drug discount platform; shared health data with Facebook, Google, advertisers.
notable_actions: FTC's first Health Breach Notification Rule enforcement (Feb 2023); $1.5M civil penalty

companies.name: Symphony Health / PRA Health Sciences
companies.description: Longitudinal patient data from pharmacy claims for pharma market research.

categories.name: Financial / Banking Data Aggregators
categories.definition: Aggregators access consumers' bank, brokerage, payroll accounts (with permission) and share data with fintech apps, lenders, financial institutions. CFPB §1033 final rule (Oct 2024) set standards; phased compliance April 2026–April 2030.

companies.name: Plaid
companies.description: Leading US bank data aggregator; 12,000+ FIs, 8,000+ apps.
notable_actions: Subject to CFPB §1033 compliance phased April 2026–April 2030

companies.name: Yodlee / Envestnet
companies.description: Pioneer bank data aggregator; data connectivity for financial wellness, wealth management, lending.

companies.name: MX Technologies
companies.description: Account aggregation, transaction enrichment, financial insights for banks and fintechs.

companies.name: Finicity
companies.description: Open banking connectivity and credit risk insights using bank account data.

companies.name: Tink
companies.description: European open banking provider expanding US presence post-Visa acquisition.

companies.name: Argyle
companies.description: Payroll/employment data connectivity for instant VOE/VOI; alternative to The Work Number.

companies.name: Pinwheel
companies.description: Payroll connectivity API for income verification, direct deposit switching, earned wage access.

companies.name: Atomic Financial
companies.description: Payroll connectivity for direct deposit switching and income verification.

companies.name: Nova Credit
companies.description: Cross-border credit bureau enabling immigrants to port international credit history into US lending.

categories.name: Telecom & ISPs
categories.definition: Carriers and ISPs see every network request, location, and communication pattern of subscribers. Despite CPNI rules, carriers historically monetized location data through reseller chains.

companies.name: AT&T
companies.description: Operates AT&T Advertising & Analytics and FirstMark; previously owned Xandr (sold to Microsoft).
notable_actions: FCC fined $57M in April 2024 for illegal location data sharing

companies.name: Verizon
companies.description: Operates Verizon Media (Yahoo) advertising platform leveraging mobile and ISP data.
notable_actions: FCC fined $46.9M in April 2024 for illegal location data sharing

companies.name: T-Mobile
companies.description: Wireless carrier; acquired Sprint.
notable_actions: FCC fined $80M in April 2024 (largest of the four) for illegal location data sharing

companies.name: Comcast / Xfinity
companies.description: ISP serving 32M broadband subscribers; operates Effectv and FreeWheel advertising.

companies.name: Charter / Spectrum
companies.description: Major cable ISP operating Spectrum Reach advertising.

companies.name: Cox
companies.description: Regional cable/ISP operating Cox Media Group advertising.

companies.name: LocationSmart
companies.description: Location aggregator that purchased real-time location data from all major carriers and resold it.
notable_actions: Subject to FCC investigation following 2018 Motherboard exposé

categories.name: Smart-TV / ACR / Connected TV
categories.definition: Smart TVs embed Automatic Content Recognition (ACR) that fingerprints on-screen content — including HDMI inputs and antennas — to identify everything viewers watch. Sold to advertisers, networks, marketers.

companies.name: Vizio / Inscape
companies.description: Inscape is Vizio's ACR data division; one of the most aggressive publishers of TV viewing data; now part of Walmart.
notable_actions: FTC $2.2M settlement (2017) for collecting smart TV data without consent

companies.name: Samba TV
companies.description: ACR data provider embedded in 24+ smart TV brands; sells audience measurement to advertisers and political campaigns.

companies.name: LG Ads
companies.hq: Seoul, South Korea / Englewood Cliffs, NJ
companies.description: LG's ACR and smart TV advertising division; sells viewing data for audience targeting.
notable_actions: Named in Dec 2025 Texas AG lawsuit

companies.name: Roku
companies.description: Streaming platform with 89M+ accounts; OneView ad platform; ACR on Roku TV licensed sets.

companies.name: Samsung Ads
companies.hq: Seoul, South Korea / Mountain View, CA
companies.description: ACR data from 50M+ Samsung smart TVs in the US.
notable_actions: Texas AG sued Dec 2025; Samsung settled in early 2026 agreeing to stop ACR data collection from Texans without explicit consent

companies.name: TiVo / Xperi
companies.description: ACR and content discovery; TiVo viewing data sold for audience analytics and ad measurement.

companies.name: Hisense
companies.description: Chinese TV manufacturer with US ACR deployments.
notable_actions: Named in Dec 2025 Texas AG lawsuit alongside Samsung, LG, Sony, TCL

companies.name: Amazon Fire TV
companies.description: Fire TV devices and Amazon-licensed TVs feed Amazon's ad ecosystem.

categories.name: Retailers with Data / Media Networks
categories.definition: Retail media networks monetize retailers' deterministic purchase data linked to authenticated customer identities. Highly valuable to CPG brands. Retail media on track to hit $106B by 2027.

companies.name: Amazon Advertising
companies.description: Largest retail media network globally; $56.2B in 2024; leverages purchase, search, Prime streaming data.

companies.name: Walmart Connect / Walmart Data Ventures
companies.description: Second-largest US retail media network; $4.4B ad revenue FY2024; acquired Vizio in 2024 for ACR integration.

companies.name: Kroger Precision Marketing / 84.51°
companies.description: Kroger's data analytics subsidiary; 60M+ household loyalty card transactions; CPG audience targeting on/offsite.

companies.name: Target Roundel
companies.description: Target's retail media network leveraging Target Circle loyalty data.

companies.name: Albertsons Media Collective
companies.description: Retail media built on Albertsons/Safeway/Vons loyalty data from 35M+ households.

companies.name: CVS Media Exchange (CMX)
companies.description: CVS Health's retail media network using pharmacy and ExtraCare loyalty data including prescription health insights.

companies.name: Walgreens Advertising Group
companies.description: Retail media using myWalgreens loyalty and pharmacy data across 9,000+ stores.

companies.name: Best Buy Ads
companies.description: Leverages consumer electronics purchase history for targeted advertising.

companies.name: Home Depot Retail Media+
companies.description: Built on Pro and DIY purchase data targeting contractor and home improvement audiences.

companies.name: Lowe's One Roof Media Network
companies.description: Home improvement retail media leveraging MyLowe's loyalty data.

companies.name: Instacart Ads
companies.description: Grocery delivery selling first-party purchase and browsing data across 1,400+ retail banners; Carrot Ads platform.

companies.name: DoorDash Ads
companies.description: Restaurant and convenience delivery selling purchase/ordering data for CPG and restaurant targeting.

companies.name: Uber Advertising
companies.description: Leverages ride and Uber Eats order data for geo-contextual and purchase-based advertising.

categories.name: Loyalty / Receipt / Panel Data
categories.definition: Aggregate purchasing data through retailer loyalty partnerships, receipt scanning apps, or opt-in consumer panels. Tied to households' complete purchase histories. Sold to CPG manufacturers, retailers, market researchers.

companies.name: Catalina Marketing
companies.description: Captures 440M loyalty cards and 11B+ shopping trips annually from 23,000+ stores at POS.

companies.name: NielsenIQ
companies.description: Retail measurement panel and POS scanner data; acquired Brandbank and merged with GfK in 2023.

companies.name: Circana
companies.description: Retail measurement, consumer behavior, loyalty data; formed by merger of IRI and NPD Group in 2023.

companies.name: Numerator
companies.description: Consumer purchase panel built on receipt-backed purchase data from verified buyers.

companies.name: Ibotta
companies.description: Cash-back rewards app monetizing purchase data from 8,000+ CPG brands and retailers.

companies.name: Fetch Rewards
companies.description: Receipt scanning app with 17M+ users; anonymized purchase data sold to CPG brands.

companies.name: Swagbucks / Prodege
companies.description: Reward platform with surveys, purchase panels, receipt data sold to market research buyers.

companies.name: InfoScout
companies.description: Original receipt-based purchase panel; now part of Numerator.

categories.name: Automotive / Connected Car Data
categories.definition: Modern vehicles generate continuous telemetry — location, speed, braking, acceleration, fuel — transmitted via OEM cellular and third-party telematics. Flows through 37+ companies identified by The Markup with minimal regulatory oversight.

companies.name: LexisNexis Risk Solutions – Telematics
companies.description: Aggregates connected vehicle data from OEM telematics; Telematics Exchange for UBI insurance; partnership with Ford announced 2021.

companies.name: Verisk / ISO
companies.description: Auto insurance analytics including driving history, claims exchange, connected vehicle data.

companies.name: Arity
companies.description: Over 1 trillion miles of driving data; embedded SDK in third-party apps (games, utilities) to harvest driving data.
notable_actions: Registered as data broker in California

companies.name: Cambridge Mobile Telematics (CMT)
companies.description: Largest telematics platform; powers DriveWell; 40B+ miles driven.

companies.name: CCC Intelligent Solutions
companies.description: Auto claims, repair, parts data platform used by 350+ insurers; vehicle history and claims analytics.

companies.name: Otonomo
companies.description: Connected vehicle data marketplace; merged with Urgently in 2022.

companies.name: Wejo
companies.description: Connected vehicle data company; SPAC in 2021 backed by GM; delisted and wound down in 2023.
notable_actions: Delisted and wound down operations in 2023

companies.name: GM / OnStar
companies.description: OnStar transmits location, driving, and diagnostic data; partners with LexisNexis for insurance data sharing.

companies.name: Ford
companies.description: Ford Telematics and Connected Services; partnered with LexisNexis to channel vehicle data to insurers.

companies.name: Smartcar
companies.description: API platform for accessing vehicle telemetry from connected cars for mobility apps, fintech, insurers.

categories.name: Real Estate / Property Data
categories.definition: Aggregate county recorder/assessor records, MLS data, mortgage filings, and derivative analytics for lenders, insurers, investors, property professionals. Three dominant platforms cover nearly all US property.

companies.name: Cotality (formerly CoreLogic)
companies.description: Largest property data platform covering 155M+ US properties; MLS aggregation, mortgage analytics, insurance risk scores, property tax, CLUE; rebranded March 2025.
notable_actions: Rebranded from CoreLogic to Cotality (March 2025)

companies.name: ATTOM Data Solutions
companies.description: 158M+ residential and commercial properties; foreclosure, ownership, AVM, neighborhood, hazard data via API.

companies.name: ICE Mortgage Technology / Black Knight
companies.description: Mortgage servicing/origination data; McDash loan performance; Data & Analytics provides property and mortgage data to lenders/investors.

companies.name: Verisk / ISO (Property)
companies.description: Property risk analytics including characteristics, reconstruction costs, catastrophe modeling.

companies.name: Zillow Group
companies.description: Consumer real estate marketplace and Zestimate AVM; licenses property and consumer engagement data to lenders/agents/advertisers.

companies.name: CoStar Group
companies.description: Commercial real estate platform covering 5M+ properties; CoStar, LoopNet, Apartments.com.

companies.name: First American Data & Analytics
companies.description: Title and property data, public records, real estate analytics for lenders and investors.

companies.name: Redfin
companies.description: Consumer real estate brokerage and listing data; sells lead data and analytics to mortgage lenders.

categories.name: Public Records / Government Data Resellers
categories.definition: Aggregate public court, property, motor vehicle, and government records into searchable databases licensed to law enforcement, government agencies, PIs, attorneys, lenders, employers. Often fall outside FCRA for 'investigative' uses.

companies.name: LexisNexis Risk Solutions
companies.description: Broadest commercial public records aggregation in US — court, criminal, property, business filings.

companies.name: Thomson Reuters CLEAR
companies.description: Investigation platform for law enforcement, government, corporate investigators; vetted access only.

companies.name: Westlaw
companies.description: Legal research database including court records, dockets, regulatory filings.

companies.name: IRB Search
companies.description: Public records aggregator and skip-tracing for debt collectors, legal pros, PIs.

companies.name: IDI / TLO
companies.description: Public records and identity verification for debt collection and legal markets.

companies.name: Tracers Information Specialists
companies.description: Public records and skip-tracing for legal and collections markets.

companies.name: Sayari Analytics
companies.description: Corporate and financial public records for sanctions screening, supply chain due diligence, financial crime.

companies.name: BRB Publications
companies.description: Aggregator of public records sources and data broker directories.

categories.name: B2B / Workplace Data
categories.definition: Compile professional profiles — title, employer, work email, direct phone, technographic and intent data — sold for sales prospecting and marketing. Fewer regulatory restrictions than consumer data.

companies.name: ZoomInfo
companies.description: 260M+ professional profiles; CRM integrations, web scraping, intent signals.
notable_actions: Multiple lawsuits over data accuracy practices

companies.name: Apollo.io
companies.description: 265M+ contacts and 60M+ companies; combines web scraping, contributors, licensed sources.

companies.name: LinkedIn Sales Navigator
companies.description: Professional network data for sales prospecting; 1B+ members globally.

companies.name: Dun & Bradstreet (D&B)
companies.description: 500M+ business entity records; D-U-N-S number system; firmographic data, supply chain risk.

companies.name: Clearbit / HubSpot Breeze Intelligence
companies.description: B2B enrichment and intent data integrated into HubSpot CRM.

companies.name: Lusha
companies.description: 100M+ professional profiles crowd-sourced from browser extension users.

companies.name: Cognism
companies.description: GDPR-compliant B2B contact data with US operations.

companies.name: 6sense
companies.description: B2B intent data using AI to predict buyer intent from anonymous research signals.

companies.name: Bombora
companies.description: B2B intent data cooperative aggregating content consumption from 5,000+ publisher sites.

companies.name: TechTarget
companies.description: B2B tech media and intent data; sells purchase intent signals from enterprise IT content.

categories.name: Education Data
categories.definition: Data from standardized testing, college admissions platforms, and ed-tech tools. FERPA restricts school-managed records, but significant flows occur through testing companies and 'school official' exception.

companies.name: College Board
companies.description: Administers SAT and AP; Student Search Service sells names, contact info, test scores, demographics to colleges/scholarships; ~170M name purchases annually.

companies.name: ACT Inc.
companies.description: ACT exam; similar student data sales for college recruitment.
notable_actions: Class action alleging student disability information was included in data sold

companies.name: PowerSchool / Naviance
companies.description: Largest K-12 SIS provider; Naviance powers college counseling for 12,000+ high schools.
notable_actions: Major data breach disclosed January 2025 affecting millions of students

companies.name: Niche.com
companies.description: College and K-12 research platform; monetizes student/parent lead generation for college enrollment marketing.

companies.name: NRCCUA / Encoura
companies.description: Enrollment management and student search; sells college-bound student data to universities.

companies.name: Hobsons
companies.description: College planning, admissions, advising software; processed student data under school licensing.

categories.name: Workforce / Employment / Background Checks
categories.definition: Regulated under FCRA — permissible purpose, adverse action notices, dispute rights. Nearly every job, tenancy, loan application passes through these systems.

companies.name: Equifax Workforce Solutions / The Work Number
companies.description: Centralized VOE/VOI database; records from 2M+ employers; 50%+ of US workers; core lending/employment infrastructure.

companies.name: Sterling
companies.description: Background screening CRA for enterprise employers; criminal, employment, education, drug screening.

companies.name: HireRight
companies.description: Pre-employment screening for global enterprises; integrates with major ATS platforms.

companies.name: Checkr
companies.description: Tech-forward background screening for gig economy, startups, enterprise; building direct payroll connections to compete with The Work Number.

companies.name: GoodHire
companies.description: FCRA-compliant background screening for SMBs.

companies.name: First Advantage
companies.description: Global background screening and identity solutions; criminal, employment, financial history.

companies.name: Accurate Background
companies.description: FCRA-compliant background screening; on CFPB's 2021 consumer reporting companies list.

categories.name: Insurance Data
categories.definition: Specialized databases of claims history, underwriting info, and risk scores affecting consumer access to and pricing of auto, home, health, life insurance. Most are specialty CRAs under FCRA.

companies.name: LexisNexis Risk Solutions – Insurance
companies.description: C.L.U.E. for auto and property claims; C.L.U.E. Auto Damage 360; driving record analytics; 98 of top 100 US personal lines carriers.

companies.name: Verisk / ISO
companies.description: Property risk scoring, catastrophe modeling, A-PLUS auto/property claims exchange, actuarial benchmarking.

companies.name: MIB Group
companies.description: Coded medical and lifestyle data for life/health/disability underwriting.

companies.name: Milliman IntelliScript
companies.description: Prescription drug history database for life/disability underwriting; sourced from PBM claims; FCRA specialty CRA.

companies.name: A-PLUS
companies.description: Personal auto/property claims database used by 900+ insurers.

categories.name: Big Tech Platforms with Data Monetization
categories.definition: Largest consumer data collectors and advertisers. Monetization models differ — Apple positions privacy as differentiator versus Google and Meta's ad-dependent architectures.

companies.name: Google / Alphabet
companies.description: Search, Chrome, Gmail, Android, Maps, YouTube; ~$237B 2024 ad revenue.

companies.name: Meta Platforms
companies.description: Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp; cross-site Pixel; ~$164B 2024 ad revenue.
notable_actions: Multiple GDPR fines in EU; ATT reduced iOS signal

companies.name: Amazon
companies.description: Purchase history, Prime Video, Alexa audio, Echo usage; ~$56B 2024 ad revenue.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.description: LinkedIn, Bing, Azure AD, Xbox, Office 365; ~$16B 2024 ad revenue; acquired Xandr (now Microsoft Advertising).

companies.name: Apple
companies.description: App Store purchases, Apple Pay, Siri, Health; Privacy-first; ATT limits third-party tracking; does not sell user data to advertisers.

categories.name: Data Exchanges / Marketplaces
categories.definition: Neutral platforms where data providers list datasets for sale/license to buyers. Unlike traditional brokers, they provide infrastructure for third-party data transactions with built-in privacy controls and cloud-native delivery.

companies.name: Snowflake Data Marketplace
companies.description: Cloud data platform with embedded marketplace; 2,000+ datasets from 500+ providers; secure cross-cloud sharing.

companies.name: AWS Data Exchange
companies.description: 3,500+ third-party datasets across industries delivered via S3, Redshift, API.

companies.name: Databricks Marketplace
companies.description: Open exchange for datasets, AI models, notebooks, apps; built on Delta Sharing.

companies.name: Narrative.io
companies.description: Data collaboration platform enabling direct buyer-seller transactions with automated governance.

companies.name: Demyst
companies.description: Enterprise data marketplace for FIs and insurers; curated third-party vendors for credit, fraud, identity.

companies.name: Crux Informatics
companies.description: Data operations platform for FIs managing external data procurement and delivery.

companies.name: Datarade
companies.description: B2B data marketplace listing 3,000+ data providers across 200+ categories.

companies.name: Bloomberg Enterprise Data
companies.description: Financial data feeds, reference data, market data for institutional investors.

companies.name: LSEG / Refinitiv Data
companies.description: Financial market data, reference data, and analytics for trading, investment, risk management.

categories.name: Mobile SDKs & App Data Collectors
categories.definition: Mobile measurement and attribution platforms embedding SDKs into thousands of apps to collect device IDs, location, in-app events, and behavioral data — processing vast raw behavioral data from hundreds of millions of devices.

companies.name: AppsFlyer
companies.description: Leading mobile attribution platform; SDK in 98%+ of top apps.

companies.name: Adjust
companies.description: Mobile measurement platform; attribution, fraud prevention, audience analytics.

companies.name: Branch
companies.description: Mobile deep linking and attribution; cross-channel identity graph.

companies.name: Kochava
companies.description: Attribution and analytics; alleged sale of sensitive location data from hundreds of millions of devices.
notable_actions: FTC sued Aug 2022; amended complaint July 2024

companies.name: Singular
companies.description: Marketing analytics and attribution aggregating ad spend and performance from 1,000+ ad networks.

companies.name: OneSignal
companies.description: Mobile/web push notification platform collecting behavioral data from engaged users.

companies.name: Braze
companies.description: Customer engagement platform processing behavioral event streams from client apps and websites.

companies.name: AirSage
companies.description: Mobile location analytics from carrier-derived location data for mobility and audience insights.

companies.name: Smaato
companies.description: Mobile advertising exchange and SSP; real-time bidding infrastructure processing consumer IDs at scale.

categories.name: Browser / Search / VPN / Antivirus Data Sales
categories.definition: Security and privacy software hold extraordinary data trust — and access. Several have exploited that trust to harvest and sell sensitive behavioral data including complete browsing histories, search queries, and network traffic.

companies.name: Avast / Jumpshot
companies.description: AV provider with 400M+ users; Jumpshot collected granular browsing data — every Google search, every URL visited — sold to advertisers, hedge funds, market researchers.
notable_actions: Jumpshot shut down January 2020 after PCMag/Motherboard investigation; FTC fined Avast $16.5M (Feb 2024); banned from selling browsing data for advertising

companies.name: Kape Technologies
companies.description: Owns ExpressVPN, Private Internet Access (PIA), CyberGhost, Intego; previously Crossrider (browser extension monetization with adware history).
notable_actions: Ongoing scrutiny over VPN data practices

companies.name: Pango Group (formerly AnchorFree)
companies.description: Operates Hotspot Shield VPN and a portfolio of privacy tools; monetizes through advertising requiring traffic analysis.

companies.name: Opera Browser
companies.description: Browser with detailed browsing data; also operates VPN and lending services; scrutiny over data flows to Chinese parent.
notable_actions: Scrutiny over data flows to Chinese parent

federal_enforcement_2023_2026.outcome: First Health Breach Notification Rule enforcement; $1.5M civil penalty
Prohibition on sale/disclosure of sensitive location data; deletion of historical data
Prohibited from selling/sharing precise location data
$16.5M fine; banned from selling browsing data for advertising
~$200M combined fines for illegal location data sharing
Amended complaint over sensitive location data; pending
Prohibited from selling sensitive location data; required destruction of historical data
Banned from selling sensitive location data; first-ever finding that RTB harvesting without bidding is unfair
Proposed FCRA expansion to treat data brokers as CRAs
Withdrew the proposed FCRA expansion rule
Court-approved $305M sale to TTAM (nonprofit led by co-founder Anne Wojcicki)
Lawsuit over ACR data collection practices
Settled — agreed to stop ACR collection from Texans without explicit consent
DELETE Act DROP platform goes live (single deletion request covering all registered brokers)
